mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing activity and rising corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If the rank IC of INDPRO 3-month change vs. 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0% signals labor market softening and weakening consumer demand, which reduces expected corporate profits and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: If UNRATE exceeds 4.0% but 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, reject.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation exceeds 2.5% year-on-year and the federal funds rate is below 2.0%, real interest rates are deeply negative, encouraging equity investment over cash and driving positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: If the joint condition is satisfied but 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 40% of occurrences, reject.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of above-trend GDP growth (quarterly annualized > 3%), further increases in retail sales trigger overheating concerns and expectations of policy tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If during high-growth periods, the rank IC of RSAFS 3-month change vs. 20-day forward returns is positive, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A surge in housing starts above 2,000 units per month indicates speculative overbuilding and impending supply glut, which depresses homebuilder sentiment and equity valuations within the following week.
failure_condition: If HOUST exceeds 2000 but the 5-day forward SPX return is positive in more than 50% of cases, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Strong job creation (PAYEMS 3-month change > 500k) combined with an unemployment rate below 4.5% signals a very tight labor market that drives wage inflation and margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: If the joint condition is met but 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, reject.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, pressures the Fed to tighten policy, compressing equity valuations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If the rank IC of CPIAUCSL 3-month change vs. 60-day forward returns is above 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in real GDP growth to an annualized rate below 1.5% signals imminent recession risk, causing a negative repricing of equities over the following quarter.
failure_condition: If GDP growth falls below 1.5% but 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of such instances, reject.
